model_narrative: We are designing a distillation system that separates a three-component feed by choosing which candidate columns to build from a superstructure of options and how much material each selected column processes. Building a column carries a fixed charge and a charge that grows with its throughput, and the heat that columns reject and absorb is matched up between hot and cold streams, drawing on outside hot utilities and cooling water where needed and paying for the heat exchange and the utilities consumed. The objective is to minimize the total annualized cost of building the selected columns together with the operating cost of the heat exchange and the hot and cold utilities.

sets.name: F_index
sets.doc: the candidate distillation columns in the design superstructure, indexed by integer; there are twenty-four candidate columns and any of them may be selected or left out of the final design

sets.name: F_subset
sets.doc: the columns that perform the first split of the three-component feed, the ones whose combined throughput must absorb the whole incoming feed; these are the first twelve candidate columns

sets.name: F_index_first_six
sets.doc: the columns that carry out the split producing the heavier intermediate product, used as the upstream side of the heavier intermediate material balance; the first six candidate columns

sets.name: F_index_last_six
sets.doc: the columns that receive and further process the heavier intermediate product, used as the downstream side of the heavier intermediate material balance; the last six candidate columns

sets.name: F_index_between_six
sets.doc: the columns that carry out the split producing the lighter intermediate product, used as the upstream side of the lighter intermediate material balance; the seventh through twelfth candidate columns

sets.name: F_index_next_between_six
sets.doc: the columns that receive and further process the lighter intermediate product, used as the downstream side of the lighter intermediate material balance; the thirteenth through eighteenth candidate columns

sets.name: cond_hot_index
sets.doc: the hot streams available to give up heat, indexed by integer; these are the twenty-four column condensers followed by three hot utility steam levels, so indices above twenty-four denote externally supplied hot utilities

sets.name: reb_cold_index
sets.doc: the cold streams that need heat, indexed by integer; these are the twenty-four column reboilers followed by one cold utility cooling-water stream, so the last index denotes the externally supplied cold utility

params.name: F_tot
params.doc: the total flow rate of the feed entering the process, in consistent flow units; a scalar equal to 250

params.name: K
params.doc: the proportionality factor that turns a column flow rate into its heat duty, in heat-duty units per flow unit; a scalar equal to 3

params.name: Stream_Matrix
params.doc: a zero or one mask marking which hot stream may feasibly deliver heat to which cold stream; equals one only when a match is thermodynamically allowed and zero otherwise, so multiplying a heat transfer by it keeps only the permitted matches

vars.name: F
vars.doc: the flow rate processed by each candidate column; zero for a column that is not built

vars.name: y
vars.doc: the build decision for each candidate column; one if the column is selected for the design and zero otherwise

vars.name: Q1
vars.doc: the heat duty of each candidate column, in heat-duty units

vars.name: QH
vars.doc: the heat made available by each hot stream, in heat-duty units

vars.name: QC
vars.doc: the heat required by each cold stream, in heat-duty units

vars.name: q
vars.doc: the heat transferred from a hot stream to a cold stream for each ordered cold-stream then hot-stream pair, in heat-duty units

constraints.name: F[j]
constraints.intent: All the feed entering the process has to be handled by the columns that perform the first separation. The combined flow processed by those first-stage columns must equal the total feed flow.

constraints.name: F[j]
constraints.intent: The heavier intermediate product made by the upstream columns has to be passed on to the columns that process it further. A fixed fraction of the combined flow from the upstream columns that produce this intermediate must equal the combined flow into the downstream columns that receive it.

constraints.name: F[j]
constraints.intent: The lighter intermediate product made by its upstream columns has to be passed on to the columns that process it further. A fixed fraction of the combined flow from the upstream columns that produce this intermediate must equal the combined flow into the downstream columns that receive it.

constraints.name: Q1[j]
constraints.intent: Each candidate column's heat duty is set by how much material it processes. For every candidate column the heat duty must equal its flow rate scaled by the duty-per-flow factor.

constraints.name: QH[j]
constraints.intent: The heat that each column's condenser makes available as a hot stream is just the column's own heat duty. For every candidate column the heat supplied by its hot stream must equal that column's heat duty.

constraints.name: F[j]
constraints.intent: A column can only carry flow if it is actually built, and even then no more than the whole feed could ever pass through it. For every candidate column the flow it processes must not exceed the total feed flow when the column is selected and must be zero when it is not.

constraints.name: QC[j]
constraints.intent: The heat that each column's reboiler needs as a cold stream is just the column's own heat duty. For every candidate column the heat required by its cold stream must equal that column's heat duty.

constraints.name: q[i,
constraints.intent: Each hot stream gives up exactly as much heat as it makes available, spread across the cold streams it is allowed to serve. For every hot stream the total heat it transfers to the permitted cold streams must equal the heat that hot stream supplies.

constraints.name: q[i,
constraints.intent: Each cold stream receives exactly as much heat as it requires, gathered from the hot streams it is allowed to draw from. For every cold stream the total heat it receives from the permitted hot streams must equal the heat that cold stream needs.

query: There's a proposal on the table to keep the train more redundant — commit at least three columns in the superstructure rather than running so lean. What does the optimizer return for total cost if we require at least three columns switched on?
expected_pyomo: model.min_cols = Constraint(expr=sum(model.y[j] for j in model.F_index) >= 3)

query: We're evaluating a debottlenecking idea where the whole A/BC bank (columns 1 through 6) is held to a combined throughput of 100 units, pushing more of the separation onto the other cut types. Plug that in and walk me through the resulting cost.
expected_pyomo: model.abc_bank_cap = Constraint(expr=sum(model.F[j] for j in model.F_index_first_six) <= 100)

query: It bugs me that the plan skips both the cheapest low-temperature A/BC column (column 1) and the widest-cut one (column 6) entirely. Couldn't we just commit both of those? Walk me through what's pulling the design the other way if we force them on together.
